points.text: Jazz Pharmaceuticals partnered with AbCellera to develop next-generation T-cell engagers for solid tumors, with T-cell engagers classified as multispecific antibodies in the deal announcement.
Johnson & Johnson reported that Talvey, a bispecific antibody, combined with Darzalex Faspro reduced the risk of death by 53% in relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma patients.
VERAXA Biotech established in vitro proof-of-concept for its novel BiTAC (bispecific T-cell engager) ADC technology platform and launched partnering discussions at BIO International Convention 2026.

VERAXA Biotech completed a business combination with Voyager Acquisition Corp to become a Nasdaq-listed company focused on next-generation cancer therapies utilizing novel bispecific T-cell engagers (TCEs) and antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs).

VERAXA Biotech secured $27.5 million in senior secured note financing and a $50 million share purchase agreement to advance its pipeline of oncology drug candidates utilizing its BiTAC bispecific platform.

Johnson & Johnson reported Phase 3 MonumenTAL-3 results for TALVEY (talquetamab), a bispecific anti-GPRC5D antibody, in combination with daratumumab for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma, showing a 72% reduction in progression or death risk versus standard care.

The MonumenTAL-3 study demonstrated a 24-month progression-free survival rate of 81.3% for the talquetamab-daratumumab combination compared to 51.2% for the standard daratumumab-pomalidomide-dexamethasone regimen.

Johnson & Johnson's bispecific portfolio achieved a third consecutive positive study outcome with MonumenTAL-3, marking the first Phase 3 trial to show superior progression-free survival for a bispecific GPRC5D combination in earlier lines of multiple myeloma treatment.

Genmab A/S — Bispecific antibodies — Corporate profile — Company explicitly identifies bispecific antibodies as a core therapeutic format alongside ADCs and immune-modulating antibodies within its portfolio of eight approved medicines and late-stage pipeline.

Memento Medicines launched with $93M Series A financing co-led by Forbion, RA Capital Management, and Avego BioScience Capital to advance MMT-205, a Tie2 agonist/VEGF inhibitor bispecific antibody for retinal diseases.

Memento Medicines secured worldwide rights to MMT-205 (formerly MT-103) via exclusive license from MabTics Co., Ltd. and Curacle Co., Ltd., with upfront cash and equity payments plus tiered royalties.
MMT-205 is a bispecific antibody designed to activate Tie2 and inhibit VEGF, targeting neovascular age-related macular degeneration (nAMD) and diabetic macular edema (DME).
Memento Medicines plans to use financing proceeds for IND-enabling studies for MMT-205, with clinical trials expected to initiate in 2027.

NovaBridge Biosciences received FDA Fast Track designation for givastomig (TJ033721/ABL111), a CLDN18.2 x 4-1BB bispecific antibody, for first-line treatment of HER2-negative, CLDN18.2-positive, and PD-L1-positive advanced or metastatic gastroesophageal adenocarcinoma.
A registrational Phase 3 trial for givastomig combined with nivolumab and chemotherapy in HER2-negative metastatic gastric cancer is planned to commence in Q4 2026.

Phase 1b data for givastomig demonstrated an objective response rate (ORR) of 77% at the 8-mg/kg dose level and 73% at the 12-mg/kg dose level in first-line HER2-negative, CLDN18.2-positive metastatic gastric cancer.

University of Virginia researchers reported that HER2 BATs (activated T-cells armed with anti-CD3 x anti-HER2 bispecific antibodies) combined with pembrolizumab were safe and well-tolerated in a Phase I/II study for metastatic breast cancer.
In the Phase I/II study of HER2 BATs and pembrolizumab, the best response was stable disease in 6 of 17 evaluable patients (35%), with a median overall survival of 21.4 months.
Emmanuel Bachy presented phase 1b/2 CO41942 data for subcutaneous mosunetuzumab (CD20/CD3 bispecific) plus lenalidomide in first-line follicular lymphoma, reporting 87% 24-month PFS and 88% CR rate.
UCB pledged up to $2.2 billion in a deal with Candid Therapeutics, a bispecific drug developer, signaling increased M&A activity in the bispecific sector.

Jazz Pharmaceuticals — AbCellera collaboration — Solid tumors (T-cell engagers/multispecific antibodies) — $56M upfront payment for two discovery programs; potential $792M per program in milestones; third program option triggers $28M.

Johnson & Johnson — Talvey (teclistamab) — Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Combination with Darzalex Faspro reduced risk of death by 53% and progression/death by 72% at 2 years vs standard regimen.

VERAXA Biotech — BiTAC platform — Solid tumors — Established in vitro proof-of-concept for novel bispecific T-cell engager (TCE) ADC technology enabling conditionally activated, AND-gated therapeutic strategies targeting dual antigens.
VERAXA Biotech — BiTAC pipeline — Solid tumors — Completed business combination with Voyager Acquisition Corp to list on Nasdaq (VRXA) and advance bispecific TCE and ADC candidates.

Johnson & Johnson — TALVEY (talquetamab) + daratumumab — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Phase 3 MonumenTAL-3 met primary endpoint: 72% reduction in progression/death risk; 24-month PFS 81.3% vs 51.2% standard; OS 89.2% vs 79.1% standard.

Genmab A/S — Bispecific antibody platform — General oncology/serious diseases — Confirmed advancement of a broad range of antibody-based therapeutic formats including bispecific antibodies as part of its 25-year strategic focus on innovative antibody medicines.

Memento Medicines — MMT-205 — Neovascular Age-Related Macular Degeneration / Diabetic Macular Edema — Secured $93M Series A financing and exclusive global license for Tie2 agonist/VEGF inhibitor bispecific antibody; IND-enabling studies underway, clinical trials planned for 2027.

NovaBridge Biosciences — Givastomig (TJ033721/ABL111) — HER2-negative, CLDN18.2-positive, PD-L1-positive gastroesophageal adenocarcinoma — FDA granted Fast Track designation; Phase 3 trial planned Q4 2026; Phase 1b ORR 77% (8 mg/kg) and 73% (12 mg/kg).

University of Virginia — HER2 BATs (anti-CD3 x anti-HER2 bispecific armed T-cells) — Metastatic breast cancer — Phase I/II trial completed; 35% stable disease rate; median OS 21.4 months; no dose-limiting toxicities observed.

Genentech/Roche — Mosunetuzumab (Lunsumio VELO) — Follicular Lymphoma — Phase 1b/2 CO41942 study: SC CD20/CD3 bispecific + lenalidomide induction achieved 90% ORR and 88% CR with 87% 24-month PFS; no ICANS or grade 5 AEs reported.
UCB — Candid Therapeutics — Bispecific pipeline — $2.2 billion deal announced to acquire bispecific drug developer, expanding UCB's oncology portfolio.

Jazz Pharmaceuticals — Expansion into solid tumor immunotherapy via multispecific antibody/T-cell engager platform — AbCellera partnership — $56M upfront; up to $1.6B+ potential value across three programs.

Johnson & Johnson — Label expansion for bispecific antibody Talvey — Teclistamab + Darzalex Faspro regimen — 53% mortality reduction in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma; data presented June 15, 2026.

VERAXA Biotech — Commercialization of bispecific T-cell engagers and ADCs — BiTAC platform pipeline — Nasdaq-listed (VRXA) post-SPAC merger; $77.5M total capital raised to advance solid tumor programs.

Johnson & Johnson — Bispecific antibody leadership in multiple myeloma — TALVEY (talquetamab) portfolio — Secured third consecutive positive Phase 3 data; advancing bispecific combinations into earlier treatment lines with MonumenTAL-3 results.

Genmab A/S — Bispecific antibody development — Broad pipeline including wholly owned programs — Maintains international presence across North America, Europe, and Asia Pacific with science powering eight approved antibody medicines and active late-stage clinical programs.

Jazz Pharmaceuticals — Expansion into solid tumor T-cell engagers — Multispecific antibody programs via AbCellera — $56M upfront; potential $4B total deal value; focus on next-gen bispecific/multispecific formats.
AbCellera — Discovery platform for multispecific T-cell engagers — Partnership with Jazz Pharmaceuticals — $56M upfront; $28M trigger for third program; up to $792M per program in milestones.

Memento Medicines — Retinal disease biologic therapy development — MMT-205 (Tie2/VEGF bispecific) — $93M Series A raised; exclusive license from MabTics/Curacle; Memento is fifth Sera Medicines subsidiary.

MabTics Co., Ltd. / Curacle Co., Ltd. — Bispecific antibody licensing — MMT-205 — Received upfront cash and equity from Memento Medicines; eligible for development, regulatory, and commercial milestones plus tiered royalties.

NovaBridge Biosciences — Development of first-in-class CLDN18.2 x 4-1BB bispecific antibody — Givastomig (TJ033721/ABL111) — FDA Fast Track designation secured; Phase 3 initiation planned Q4 2026; Phase 1b data supports robust efficacy in gastric cancer.

University of Virginia — Clinical evaluation of bispecific antibody-armed T-cell therapy — HER2 BATs (anti-CD3 x anti-HER2) — Phase I/II study in metastatic breast cancer demonstrated safety and stable disease in 35% of patients.
Candid Therapeutics — Bispecific drug development — Oncology bispecific pipeline — Acquired by UCB in a deal valued up to $2.2 billion.

Genentech/Roche — CD20/CD3 bispecific commercialization — Mosunetuzumab — Phase 3 MorningLyte trial initiated to compare SC mosunetuzumab + lenalidomide vs standard immunochemotherapy in first-line follicular lymphoma.

Talvey (teclistamab) — Mechanism: Bispecific T-cell engager (BCMA x CD3) — Phase: Late-stage/Label expansion study — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Comparator: Standard of care — Results: 53% reduction in death risk; 72% reduction in progression/death risk at 2 years.

BiTAC platform — Mechanism: Bispecific T-cell engager (TCE) with AND-gated dual-antigen targeting for conditional activation — Phase: Preclinical (in vitro proof-of-concept) — Population: Solid tumors with non-exclusive cancer markers — Results: Demonstrated ability to target cancer cells precisely while sparing healthy cells.

TALVEY (talquetamab) — Mechanism: Bispecific anti-GPRC5D antibody — Phase: 3 (MonumenTAL-3) — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma (earlier lines) — Comparator: Daratumumab + pomalidomide + dexamethasone — Results: 72% risk reduction in progression/death; 81.3% PFS at 24 months vs 51.2%.

Talquetamab — Mechanism: Bispecific GPRC5D-targeting antibody — Phase: 3 (MonumenTAL-3) — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma in earlier treatment lines — Comparator: Daratumumab, pomalidomid, dexamethason (DPd) — Results: 81.3% PFS at 24 months vs 51.2%; 89.2% OS vs 79.1%; 72% risk reduction in progression/death.

Genmab A/S pipeline — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody format — Phase: Late-stage clinical — Population: Cancer and serious diseases — Status: Advancing wholly owned programs with goal of delivering transformative medicines; specific trial data not detailed in source.

T-cell engagers (multispecific antibodies) — Mechanism: Redirect T-cells to solid tumor antigens — Phase: Preclinical/discovery — Population: Solid tumor indications — Status: Early-stage research initiated under Jazz/AbCellera deal; aims to overcome limited success in solid tumors.

MMT-205 — Mechanism: Tie2 receptor agonism combined with VEGF inhibition — Phase: Preclinical/IND-enabling — Population: Retinal vascular disease (nAMD, DME) — Comparator: Anti-VEGF monotherapies — Results: Preclinical models show greater Tie2 activation and improved retinal vasculature integrity vs current therapies.

Givastomig — Mechanism: CLDN18.2 x 4-1BB bispecific antibody — Phase: 1b (expansion) — Population: First-line HER2-negative, CLDN18.2-positive, PD-L1-positive metastatic gastric cancer — Comparator: Historical benchmarks — Results: ORR 77% (8 mg/kg), median PFS 16.9 months (8 mg/kg).

HER2 BATs — Mechanism: Activated T-cells armed with anti-CD3 x anti-HER2 bispecific antibodies — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Metastatic breast cancer (any HER2 status, ≥2 prior lines) — Comparator: None (single arm) — Results: 35% stable disease, median OS 21.4 months, no dose-limiting toxicities.

Mosunetuzumab — Mechanism: CD20×CD3 bispecific antibody — Phase: 1b/2 (CO41942) — Population: First-line follicular lymphoma with high tumor burden — Comparator: Historical GALLIUM study data — Results: 90% ORR, 88% CR, 87% 24-month PFS; manageable safety with low-grade CRS, no ICANS.

Jazz Pharmaceuticals / AbCellera — Strategic collaboration for multispecific antibody discovery — Two initial programs + one optional third — $56M upfront; $28M option trigger; up to $792M per program in development/regulatory milestones.
VERAXA Biotech / Voyager Acquisition Corp — Business Combination — Nasdaq listing (VRXA) — Closed merger transaction; shareholders approved on March 12, 2026, with trading commencing June 11, 2026.
VERAXA Biotech — Capital raise — BiTAC pipeline — Secured $27.5M senior secured note financing and $50M share purchase agreement to fund bispecific TCE and ADC development.
Memento Medicines — $93M Series A financing — Co-led by Forbion, RA Capital Management, Avego BioScience Capital; participation by Sanofi Ventures and Samsara BioCapital to fund MMT-205 development.

Memento Medicines / MabTics & Curacle — Exclusive license agreement — MMT-205 — Worldwide rights transferred; deal includes upfront cash/equity plus future milestones and tiered royalties on net sales.
UCB / Candid Therapeutics — Acquisition — Bispecific oncology assets — Deal value up to $2.2 billion announced to secure Candid's bispecific drug development capabilities.
